conditions: The request explicitly names Bright Data or requires anti-bot/structured page extraction.
Bright Data MCP tools or MCP-first workflow is available or requested.
policies.why: Bright Data MCP is the most specific route for agent-layer Bright Data scrape/extract/search workflows; the parent skill only routes when child selection is unclear.

suppressed.reason: Parent router is less specific after MCP workflow is clear.
CLI scrape is fallback when MCP is unavailable or CLI is explicitly requested.
Generic browsing does not provide Bright Data anti-bot or structured extraction routing.

fallbacks.condition: Bright Data MCP is unavailable but local bdata CLI is configured.
Bright Data auth, zone, budget, or CLI/MCP setup is missing.

safety_notes: Do not run live scrape/search solely to prove routing.
Respect current network and tool availability.
policies.review_notes: Initial policy from skill-router v1 acceptance checks.

conditions: The task is planning/specification, not execution.
Requirements are clear enough to become an executable plan.
No explicit hybrid team plugin workflow is required.
policies.why: Use spec2plan for clear requirements-to-plan conversion; use interview/idea skills only before requirements are stable.

suppressed.reason: Hybrid team workflow is plugin-specific and should not displace local spec2plan unless requested.
Only for rough concepts targeting the hybrid team `.codex/specs` workflow.
Execution skill, not plan creation.

fallbacks.condition: Who, why, success criteria, scope, or constraints are missing.
Direction is still uncertain and needs option exploration.

safety_notes: Do not spawn workers while only planning unless explicit delegation is authorized.

conditions: An existing plan artifact is the execution source.
The user asks to implement, execute, finish, or complete the plan.
policies.why: plan2do owns execution of existing plan.md artifacts with verification, review, bounded rework, and artifact reporting.

suppressed.reason: Planning is already complete unless the plan is invalid or missing.
Worker coordination requires explicit delegation and file-disjoint scopes.
Use only when orchestration becomes long/noisy/risky or multi-branch, not for normal plan execution.

fallbacks.condition: The plan is missing, stale, contradictory, or not executable.
A dedicated acceptance review is requested after implementation.

safety_notes: Preserve unrelated user changes; do not spawn agents unless explicitly requested.

conditions: The user asks for a general artifact/code/spec/plan/workflow review.
The request is not specifically over-engineering-only or skill-execution trace debugging.
policies.why: reviewer owns generic quality gates, evidence receipts, lite/heavy routing, and PASS/REVISE/BLOCK verdicts.

suppressed.reason: Use only for over-engineering/deletion-focused diff review.
Use only for auditing whether a skill execution helped or harmed.
Use only inside the hybrid Codex team workflow.

fallbacks.condition: The requested review criterion is over-engineering or deletion only.
The artifact is a skill execution trace or skill behavior audit.

safety_notes: Heavy review defaults to isolation only when subagent delegation is allowed by current policy.

conditions: The artifact under review is skill behavior, skill execution quality, or a skill optimization candidate.
policies.why: debug-skill owns evidence-backed audits of whether a specified skill helped or harmed a task.

suppressed.reason: Generic artifact review is fallback; debug-skill has the domain-specific trace rubric.
Tokenless optimizes or gates skill changes after audit findings exist.
Creator edits skills; it does not audit live execution traces by default.

fallbacks.condition: A generic PASS/REVISE/BLOCK gate is requested for the audit report.
The accepted fix is a skill token/cost/production-gate update.

safety_notes: Do not modify the audited skill unless the user separately requests implementation.

conditions: A Python environment, dependency, import, resolver, interpreter, or binary wheel blocker appears while coding/testing/running scripts.
policies.why: uv-mirror-env is a mandatory interrupt for Python env/dependency blockers and must repair the environment before ordinary debug or TDD continues.

suppressed.reason: Generic debugging resumes after env repair.
TDD cannot prove behavior while dependencies/imports are broken.
Skill requires uv-managed, mirror-aware recovery workflow.

fallbacks.condition: The env is repaired and failures persist.
Behavior change or regression tests are needed after repair.

safety_notes: Do not ask the user before local safe uv repair paths are exhausted.

conditions: The request concerns context quality, task switching, compaction, handoff, or token-saving reply style.
policies.why: context-engineering governs context quality and compaction decisions; handoff and caveman are narrower child routes.

suppressed.reason: Use only when the requested output is a handoff document.
Use only for terse reply/token-saving style, not context governance.
Use only when session orchestration/firewalling is required.

fallbacks.condition: The user asks to produce a handoff artifact.
The user asks for terse mode, token savings, or compressed responses.

safety_notes: Summaries are navigation, not evidence; re-read authoritative files for risky decisions.

conditions: The request asks for concrete git state work such as dirty-tree inspection, commit, branch, worktree, PR, release, tag, version bump, or history organization.
The primary goal is not context isolation or multi-session orchestration.
policies.why: git-workflow-and-versioning owns concrete git hygiene, commits, branches, worktrees, PRs, tags, releases, and history organization.

suppressed.reason: Conductor owns context/firewall/orchestration, not ordinary git operations.

route.name: raw git commands without git skill

suppressed.reason: Git hygiene matters when changing repo state.

fallbacks.condition: Work becomes long, noisy, multi-turn, parallel, risky, dependency-heavy, or multi-branch.

safety_notes: Never commit, branch, or rewrite history without explicit user request.

conditions: The request asks about code structure, symbol flow, callers/callees, impact, or broad repo understanding.
policies.why: CodeGraph owns indexed code intelligence for symbol/call/impact questions; lean-ctx remains the cached read/search/shell support route.

suppressed.reason: lean-ctx is support/fallback for source reads/search, not primary graph reasoning when CodeGraph applies.
Broad grep/read loops duplicate indexed codegraph work.

fallbacks.condition: CodeGraph is unavailable, stale for edited files, or the task is only cached read/search/shell.
No indexed code or lean-ctx tool is available.

evidence.value: lean-ctx tool metadata in active session

safety_notes: After edits, respect codegraph staleness banners and re-read changed files if needed.

conditions: The request needs external web information and may match generic web browsing or Bright Data.
policies.why: Generic official-source browsing and citations use web.run unless the user explicitly asks Bright Data or anti-bot/structured SERP/scrape is required.

suppressed.reason: Bright Data is over-specific for ordinary browsing/citation tasks without anti-bot or SERP/extraction requirements.
Use only when Bright Data SERP/Search API or Discover is requested/needed.

fallbacks.condition: SERP API, Discover, anti-bot search, or Bright Data is explicitly requested.
The topic is OpenAI products/APIs/Codex docs.

evidence.value: web.run tool metadata in active session

safety_notes: Use primary/official sources for technical docs; browse when facts may have changed.

conditions: A request would spawn or coordinate agents but the user has not explicitly authorized delegation in the current task.
policies.why: Multi-agent spawning is mode/tool gated and requires explicit user delegation; planning may prepare scopes but must not launch workers.

suppressed.reason: Requires explicit delegation and file-disjoint worker scopes.
Can route orchestration but does not override missing spawn authorization.
Forbidden without explicit delegation.

fallbacks.condition: Prepare a worker-ready plan without spawning agents.
The user explicitly authorizes multi-agent delegation.

safety_notes: State the blocker rather than silently downgrading to spawned work.

conditions: The active session exposes lean-ctx tools and the task is local read/search/tree/shell rather than file modification.
policies.why: lean-ctx is the mandated compact route for cached reads, searches, trees, and shell commands when available.

suppressed.reason: Use only when lean-ctx is unavailable or raw PTY/escalation behavior is specifically required.
Use lean-ctx equivalents for token savings and cached context.

fallbacks.condition: lean-ctx is unavailable or the command needs PTY/session semantics not supported by lean-ctx.

evidence.value: lean-ctx tool metadata in active session

safety_notes: Do not use shell commands to edit files; route edit decisions to structural-edit and strict text fallback execution to apply_patch.

conditions: The task requires manual file creation, modification, move, or delete.
policies.why: structural-edit is the default manual edit decision route; apply_patch is the strict text fallback and execution tool only when structural-edit allows it.

suppressed.reason: Shell writes can bypass patch grammar, anchors, and diff safety.
lean-ctx replaces read/search/shell only, not manual writes.

fallbacks.condition: structural-edit classifies the edit as tiny, unique, low-risk strict text fallback.
The project owns the output via a generator or formatter.

safety_notes: Preserve unrelated dirty work and inspect diffs after edits.
Run structural-edit route classification before choosing apply_patch for manual edits.

conditions: A route names `$plan-grill`, but no active skill named `plan-grill` exists in the session skill list.
policies.why: `$plan-grill` is a stale route name; spec2plan replaces planning-and-task-breakdown and plan hardening for executable plans.

suppressed.reason: Missing from active skills.
Conductor may mention the stale route but should not be selected when the target skill is absent.

fallbacks.condition: The user only asks to review an existing plan, not convert or harden it.
The user asks to execute the plan.

safety_notes: Do not create or install a missing skill just to satisfy a stale route name.

conditions: The requested review criterion is specifically over-engineering, deletion, simplification, or YAGNI.
policies.why: ponytail-review owns diff-focused over-engineering/deletion review and complements correctness review.

suppressed.reason: Generic correctness/security review is fallback, not primary for deletion-focused review.
Parent mode is broader; child review skill owns diff review.

fallbacks.condition: Correctness, security, tests, or artifact acceptance must also be quality-gated.
The user asks for whole-repo over-engineering audit instead of diff review.

safety_notes: Do not apply simplifications unless the user asks for implementation.

conditions: The request asks about OpenAI products/APIs/Codex docs and OpenAI docs MCP tools are unavailable or not exposed in the active tool list.
policies.why: openai-docs owns OpenAI product/API/Codex documentation routing; if docs MCP is unavailable, fallback browsing must stay on official OpenAI domains.

suppressed.reason: OpenAI docs require official sources unless the user requests otherwise.
Bright Data is not required for official OpenAI docs unless explicitly requested.

fallbacks.condition: OpenAI docs MCP is unavailable or insufficient.
Neither docs MCP nor official OpenAI browsing is available.

safety_notes: Browse for current OpenAI product facts; restrict fallback to official OpenAI domains.

conditions: Two or more active skills or tools share an apparent name or capability and no more specific policy matches.
policies.why: Exact duplicate names are unsafe to auto-resolve without a source locator, plugin prefix, or user-named target.

suppressed.reason: Unqualified duplicate can load the wrong workflow.

fallbacks.condition: A fully-qualified plugin skill name is available and matches the user request.
No source locator or prefix disambiguates the duplicate.

safety_notes: Ask for disambiguation instead of guessing when exact duplicate names remain.

conditions: More than one skill/tool plausibly matches the same request, or routing involves stale routes, missing MCP tools, mode-gated tool calls, duplicate names, or parent/child skill overlap.
No higher-priority current instruction already resolves the conflict.
policies.why: Global soft hook makes conflict routing deterministic without adding overhead to single clear skill/tool matches.

suppressed.reason: First-match behavior can load the wrong workflow when candidates conflict.
Ad hoc tool selection can bypass saved governance policy.

fallbacks.condition: Only one skill/tool clearly matches.
All candidate routes are blocked by tool availability, approval mode, safety, or missing requirements.

safety_notes: Soft hook is advisory and must not override system/developer/user instructions or current tool constraints.
Do not invoke skill-router for single clear matches.
policies.review_notes: Added in v2 after user approved global soft hook.
